open_followups.title: fix verification_data loader :meta::jsonb SQL cast
open_followups.why_not_debugger: P00-S02-T003 ya esta committed y mergeado en main; el bug del cast :meta::jsonb solo aflora ahora porque T001 crea las tablas auth que activan el code path del loader. No es fix in-scope de T001 (write_set distinto: backend/app/verification_data/loader.py).

open_followups.title: fix dev-restart.profile.sh verification-data bootstrap source path
open_followups.why_not_debugger: Bug is in scripts/dev-restart.profile.sh, not in the P01-S02-T002 sign-in write set (backend/app/auth/**). Debugger inside T002 cannot touch dev tooling without expanding Write set / Conflict group, which is exactly what FU exists for. The bug exists since the profile was first added and was just unmasked because T002 was the first slice to use --reset after the verification_data loader fix (P00-S02-T004) landed; not a regression introduced by sign-in.

open_followups.title: generate ≥32-byte JWT dev key + default ENABLE_VERBOSE_LOGGING in dev .env
open_followups.why_not_debugger: The .env file in the repo (not .env.example) is developer-local state, outside the T002 Write set. The placeholder 'replace-with-dev-key' was set by setup-from-scratch and never rotated; tokens.py correctly emits a warning at startup but cannot fail-hard without breaking other devs. Fix belongs to a dedicated config-hygiene slice that generates a real ≥32-byte random dev key, wires ENABLE_VERBOSE_LOGGING=true as the dev default, and updates setup-from-scratch.sh to provision both. None of this fits the sign-in canonical Write set.

open_followups.title: make bootstrap_three_docs.py --refresh preserve closer-set task status
open_followups.why_not_debugger: Bug lives in the orchestrator framework (.claude/bin/bootstrap_three_docs.py + supporting modules), not in any app slice Write set. The bug only manifests when bootstrap_three_docs.py --refresh runs on a registry that has tasks at status=done from recent closers — refresh regenerates task records and resets the status to ready_for_close. I patched the registry manually for T002 (commit 570b702) but the root cause persists for any future refresh. Debugger inside T002 cannot fix orchestrator framework code without expanding Write set across the entire .claude/ static config.

open_followups.title: fix dev-restart db_health race: wait for host TCP, not container-internal pg_isready
open_followups.why_not_debugger: T008 acceptance is narrowly the seed --source path (lines 347-360 of scripts/dev-restart.profile.sh). The race lives in db_health() (lines 153-157) which runs pg_isready INSIDE the postgres container; meanwhile alembic upgrade head connects from the HOST at 127.0.0.1:5432 — container-internal-ready and host-TCP-forward-ready are different timings under Rancher. Two-consecutive --reset can race between compose down -v + compose up + alembic. T008 fix did not introduce this race (seed block runs after alembic), but T008's hard-fail correctly surfaces it as RESET2_EXIT=1. Bundling a wait-for-host-tcp into T008 would expand its responsibility from one bug to two unrelated bugs (KISS/YAGNI violation per .claude/rules/01-non-negotiables.md). Needs its own slice with separate acceptance: db_health (or a new wait_for_host_tcp helper) must verify host TCP 5432 reachability with credentials before returning success.

open_followups.title: fix refresh cookie Path mismatch (/auth vs /api/v1/auth) across sign-in setter + refresh/logout consumers
open_followups.why_not_debugger: Fix changes _helpers._set_refresh_cookie path attribute, which is consumed identically by T002 sign-in (setter), T003 refresh (consumer), and T004 logout (consumer). A coordinated fix must re-verify T002 sign-in cookie still arrives at /api/v1/auth/refresh and /api/v1/auth/logout, T003 refresh still revokes+rotates, and reconcile TECHNICAL_GUIDE §10.2 (which does not currently pin a Path attribute; only HttpOnly Secure SameSite=Lax). Debugger inside T004 alone cannot mutate T002/T003 contracts and would silently change cookie attrs across the auth surface, which is the exact pattern this triage rule prevents.

open_followups.title: Backend/infra: configure cross-origin between frontend (:5173) and backend (:8000) — vite proxy or CORSMiddleware
open_followups.why_not_debugger: CORS configuration is outside T001's Write set (frontend/src/features/auth/** + frontend/src/app/router.tsx). The two clean fixes both live elsewhere: (a) frontend/vite.config.ts server.proxy reverse-proxying /api → http://localhost:8000 (recommended; same pattern as nginx/cloudfront in prod, BFF-style same-origin); (b) backend/app/main.py registering CORSMiddleware(allow_origins=['http://localhost:5173'], allow_credentials=true, allow_headers=['authorization','content-type','x-request-id'], allow_methods=['GET','POST','PATCH','PUT','DELETE','OPTIONS']). Either path requires Write set expansion outside T001 plus a TECHNICAL_GUIDE amendment to spec the cross-origin / reverse-proxy strategy. Debugger inside T001 cannot legitimately touch any of these paths without violating Write set discipline. T001 is the slice that EXPOSES the gap (first frontend slice making cross-origin XHR from a real browser); it does not introduce the gap.
